description:
You are acting as a database reliability engineer who must confirm basic network health before kicking-off the nightly backup job.  
Your objective: produce a one-off diagnostic log that captures a loopback connectivity test.

1. Create the directory  
   /home/user/logs  
   if it does not already exist.  (Keep any parent directories exactly as written.)

2. Run a single network-diagnostic command that sends **three** ICMP echo requests to the local loopback interface.

3. Write the *complete* command output to the file  
   /home/user/logs/loopback_ping.log  
   preceded by the two header lines shown below (use a single space after each colon):

   TIMESTAMP: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS  
   COMMAND: ping -c 3 127.0.0.1  

   • Replace YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS with the current local system time obtained from the shell (use the exact format shown, 24-hour clock).  
   • After these two header lines, append the unedited diagnostic output generated by the command itself.  
   • Ensure there is a Unix newline (LF) after each line. The file must therefore start with exactly these two header lines, each on its own line, followed immediately by the ping output.

4. Do **not** print the file contents to the terminal after creation; simply ensure that the file exists and is populated per the format above.

The automated tests will:
• Check that /home/user/logs/loopback_ping.log exists.  
• Verify the first two lines begin with “TIMESTAMP:” and “COMMAND:” respectively and that the COMMAND line ends with “ping -c 3 127.0.0.1”.  
• Confirm the body of the file (ping output) contains “0% packet loss”.  
If any of these conditions fail, the task is considered incomplete.
truth:
Expected artefact:
File: /home/user/logs/loopback_ping.log

Verification details:
1. The file must exist and be non-empty.
2. Line-1 regex: ^TIMESTAMP:\s+\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}\s+\d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}\n$
3. Line-2 regex: ^COMMAND:\s+ping\s+-c\s+3\s+127\.0\.0\.1\n$
4. Somewhere after line-2 the text “0% packet loss” must appear (anywhere in the remaining content).

Directory /home/user/logs does not exist before the task begins; the agent must create it.
name: task_000000_d69e2d33
